idea: Multi-Latent Attention (MLA): low-rank KV compression via latent bottleneck, reduces VRAM allowing larger batch or model
evidence: DeepSeek-V2/V3 paper; 93% KV cache reduction; multiple open-source implementations available
notes: Compresses K,V through shared low-rank latent. Frees VRAM headroom. Main benefit is inference but training VRAM savings could allow batch_size=8 or deeper model.

idea: NorMuon: normalize Muon update by applying norm step to attention params at stacked shape before reshape
evidence: modded-nanogpt PR#144 (-15 steps/-0.7s WR); note: "adding negligible value to gates and attention" per discussion
notes: Small change to Muon step. Low risk, low reward. Try after architecture changes.

idea: Attention Residuals (scalar softmax over all prior layer hiddens) replacing current resid+x0 mix: per-layer learnable weights over [h_0..h_{l-1}] with softmax
evidence: Kimi/Moonshot arxiv 2603.15031 Mar2026 'Attention Residuals' mitigates PreNorm dilution + d16->d20 depth success proves signal-dilution is our bottleneck
notes: Generalizes existing resid_lambdas+x0_lambdas (2-term mix) to d+1 term softmax-weighted mix. Adds d(d+1)/2=210 scalar params. Activation mem: d * B*T*n_embd bf16 ~252MB — fits in ~2GB headroom. Risk: may converge to current pattern; softmax may be too soft at init.

idea: GatedNorm: insert learnable sigmoid gate after RMSNorm in transformer blocks (y = sigmoid(gate) * rmsnorm(x))
evidence: arxiv 2601.22966 Jan2026 'Unified View of Attention/Residual Sinks' GatedNorm mitigates sinks +2pt gain
notes: Adds n_embd params per norm site. Our norm() is called 3x per block (pre-attn, post-attn, pre-mlp) plus final. ~4*20*768=61k params. Direct fix to the sink problem + improves quantization robustness.

idea: Aggressive SWA pattern SSSSL ratio 4:1 + window 256->128 (was SSSL 3:1 256)
evidence: Qwen3.5 Feb2026 uses 5:1 ratio with 128 window. RATTENTION paper 2506.15545 'minimal window size' direction.
notes: At d20 SSSL gives 5 L-layers SSSSL gives 4 L-layers — saves compute per long-window layer. Narrower window is cheaper; may free throughput for marginal capacity. Wider window (512) was already tried and worse (81332ca).
